trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: 'Walk now in my light. When the rest of the world closes its eyes, you will still see.' — Iona [Scryfall] — The closing-eyes imagery directly references the MTG lore explanation for why Zendikar's angels wear halos down over their eyes: after the Eldrazi's first awakening, the angels 'closed their eyes' to the Eldrazi's atrocities, symbolizing powerlessness and complicity. [WebSearch: MTG Fandom, Iona character page via WebSearch snippet] Iona's gift of sight carries a pointed moral weight in that context.
sources: WebSearch: Iona MTG Fandom page snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Iona, Shield of Emeria is one of the greater archangels of Zendikar — described as gargantuan, six-winged, with pupil-less glowing white eyes and no halo across her own eyes, unlike other Zendikari angels. [WebSearch: Iona MTG Fandom page snippet] The aura depicted in this card shows her blessing passing to a mortal warrior rather than Iona herself.
sources: WebSearch: Iona MTG Fandom page snippet

trivia_bullets.text: A 3W Aura giving +2/+2, vigilance, and 'block an additional creature each combat.' The multi-block clause is mechanically rare in white Auras and synergizes strongly with already-large defensive creatures. EDHREC rank 18736. [Scryfall]

related_cards.name: Iona, Shield of Emeria
related_cards.note: The named source of the blessing; banned in Legacy

related_cards.name: Iona's Judgment
related_cards.note: Same Iona-named card cycle in the inventory

related_cards.note: Fellow Zendikari archangel from the same plane and era
